description:
You are acting as an integration-developer who just finished testing an e-commerce
    “Orders” REST endpoint.  
    The QA team has saved a sample response payload for you in the file:

        /home/user/api_responses/orders.json

    Your job is to generate a very small, human-readable report from that JSON.
    Follow these rules exactly:

    1. Create the directory  /home/user/work/output  
       (it does **not** exist yet).

    2. Transform /home/user/api_responses/orders.json into a comma-separated-value
       file named:

        /home/user/work/output/orders_report.csv

    3. The CSV must contain **only** the following three columns, in this exact
       order and with this exact header row (case-sensitive):

           order_id,customer_id,total

    4. Every subsequent line must contain the corresponding values for each order
       from the JSON, in the same order as they appear in the source file.  
       Example of final layout (including the header):

           order_id,customer_id,total
           1001,C001,123.45
           1002,C002,67.89
           1003,C003,250.0

       • Values are separated only by commas (no extra spaces).  
       • Make sure the file ends with a single trailing newline.  
       • No additional columns, headers, or footers are allowed.

    5. After creating the file, display (print to stdout) the entire CSV so
       a colleague can visually confirm the result.

    NOTE: The automated test will verify
          a. that /home/user/work/output/orders_report.csv exists  
          b. that its contents **exactly** match the layout above, including the
             header row, field order, comma placement and trailing newline.
truth:
Initial files present before the task starts:
      • /home/user/api_responses/orders.json

        [
          { "order_id": 1001, "customer_id": "C001", "total": 123.45, "status": "shipped" },
          { "order_id": 1002, "customer_id": "C002", "total": 67.89,  "status": "processing" },
          { "order_id": 1003, "customer_id": "C003", "total": 250.0,   "status": "delivered"  }
        ]

    Expected file after successful task completion:
      • /home/user/work/output/orders_report.csv

        order_id,customer_id,total
        1001,C001,123.45
        1002,C002,67.89
        1003,C003,250.0
        ␊   ← single trailing newline character (no extra blank lines)

    Directory permissions:
      • /home/user/work will be writable by the agent.
